_comment: DepthViT-22M tier-4 balanced-scaling point (L=18, k_factor=196 -> hidden_dim=392, 22,046,777 params, verified to the exact integer by building the nn.Module) on HLS4ML LHC jets 150p — cold-start, no pretraining. This tier deliberately steps off the pure-k axis used by the 164K/1M/5M tiers: k alone to 22M (k=233, L=12) leaves HAP at ~40% of the model and a lopsided hidden_dim=466 over only 2 channels. Adding depth (L 12->18) is a counterweight that holds HAP in proportion (28.1%) and keeps the channel-asymmetric mechanism at ~22%, letting the extra budget flow into standard encoder/FFN growth the way a normally-scaled transformer does. mlp_dim stays PINNED at 768 (same as every other tier) so the tier-1..3 pure-k story is unchanged. Component split at this config: chan-attn 22.1% / FFN 49.4% / HAP 28.1%. NOTE: the ViT-Small matched-parameter target is NOT yet confirmed against the actual param counter — treat 22.05M as the DepthViT tier-4 point, and reconcile the exact ViT-Small match number separately before claiming a matched comparison.

model._comment: patch=10 -> 10x10 token grid. hap_window=5 divides cleanly (2x2 windows). 18 depth-wise + 4 HAP = 22 blocks, interleaved (DDDDD-H)(DDDDD-H)(DDDD-H)(DDDD-H) by the group_size=18//4 rule. k_factor=196 -> hidden_dim=392 -> 22,046,777 params (verified). D//k = 392//196 = 2 = in_channels. ONLY changes vs the 5M/164K/1M baselines: k_factor (90->196) and n_layers (12->18). Everything else (mlp_dim, num_hap_layers, hap settings) held identical.
